name: Senseonics Holdings, Inc.
business: The company is a medical technology company that develops and commercializes implantable continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems (Eversense E3 and Eversense 365) for people with diabetes. It sells primarily through distributors and strategic fulfillment partners to healthcare providers and patients. Revenue includes sales of sensor packs, transmitter packs, and procedure fees.

exposures.sentence: We are a medical technology company focused on the development and commercialization of a long-term, implantable continuous glucose monitoring ( CGM ) system
Our mobile app is a software application that runs on both platforms; iOS mobile devices, including iPhones, iPads and Apple Watches, and Android mobile devices.

catalysts.event: Launch of Eversense 365 in European Territories
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, the Company obtained CE Mark approval for Eversense 365 and expects to launch Eversense 365 in the European Territories by the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Signing of local asset purchase agreements for European assets
catalysts.sentence: We expect to enter into a series of asset purchase agreements to acquire certain additional assets related to Ascensia's commercial Eversense activities in the European Territories on or before March 31, 2026.

The closing of each of the European Asset Purchases will be subject to the terms and conditions set forth in the Master Asset Purchase Agreement and the Local Purchase Agreements, and are expected to close on or before June 30, 2026.
We expect to complete the trial in 2026 to support a 510(k) submission to the FDA.
We aim to launch the Gemini and Freedom systems by the fourth quarter of 2027 and fourth quarter of 2028, respectively.
We aim to launch the Gemini and Freedom systems by the fourth quarter of 2027 and fourth quarter of 2028, respectively.

tamClaims.claim: An estimated 589 million people worldwide had diabetes as of the date of the report.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the 2025 International Diabetes Federation Atlas, an estimated 589 million people worldwide had diabetes as of the date of the report.

tamClaims.claim: The number of people with diabetes worldwide is estimated to reach 853 million by 2050.
tamClaims.sentence: The number of people with diabetes worldwide is estimated to reach 853 million by 2050, driven primarily by growth in type 2 diabetes and due to various reasons, including an aging population, changes in dietary trends primarily as the result of increased urbanization, and a higher prevalence of the disease in younger people.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 300 million people in the United States may have coverage and access to Eversense.
tamClaims.sentence: To date, approximately 300 million people in the United States may have coverage and access to Eversense via commercial (for example, UnitedHealthcare) or government (for example, Medicare) payors.
